summary_sections.title: Key Concepts: River Systems & Landforms
summary_sections.content_html:
Rivers shape landscapes through erosion, transport, and deposition, creating distinctive landforms:

  • Upper course: steep gradient, fast flow, V-shaped valleys, waterfalls, rapids
  • Middle course: moderate gradient, wider valley, meanders begin to form
  • Lower course: gentle gradient, wide floodplain, meandering river, oxbow lakes, deltas
  • Key features:
    • Meanders: S-shaped curves; erosion on the outer bank, deposition on the inner bank
    • Oxbow lakes: formed when a meander loop is cut off from the main river
    • Floodplain: flat area adjacent to a river that floods periodically, receiving fertile sediment
    • Drainage basin (watershed): the total area drained by a river and its tributaries
